nodes.name: LC
nodes.summary: LC表示 IUCN 无危等级，当前关联8208种鸟类，如鸵鸟、鸸鹋、南方鶴鴕等8208种鸟类。

nodes.name: 红嘴鹲
nodes.summary: 红嘴鹲（Phaethon aethereus）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联1种鸟类，如红嘴热带鸟。

nodes.name: 红嘴热带鸟
nodes.summary: 红嘴热带鸟（Red-billed tropicbird，学名 Phaethon aethereus），分类上属于鸏形目，并归入鹲科，在图谱中标记为无危（LC）物种，已关联12个分布地点节点，主要威胁包括小种群风险。
nodes.shortSummary: 红嘴热带鸟（学名：Phaethon aethereus）为鹲科熱帶鳥屬的鸟类，俗名短尾热带鸟、红嘴鹲。是熱帶鳥的一種，為熱帶海洋中三種密切相關的海鳥之一。表面上看起來像燕鷗，它主要是白色羽毛，翅膀和背部有一些黑色斑紋，有黑色的面具，正如其俗名所指出的那樣，擁有紅色的喙。大多數成…

nodes.name: 小种群风险
nodes.summary: 种群规模过小导致遗传多样性下降和脆弱性上升。

nodes.name: 塞内加尔
nodes.summary: 塞内加尔是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联88种鸟类，包括White-throated francolin、Ahanta spurfowl、绿冠蕉鹃等88种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 鸏形目
nodes.summary: 鸏形目（Phaethontiformes）是图谱中的目级分类节点，当前关联3种鸟类，如红尾热带鸟、白尾热带鸟、红嘴热带鸟。

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: indicus is found in waters off Pakistan, western India, southwestern Sri Lanka, the Horn of Africa and Arabian Peninsula. The subspecies is also a rare but regular vagrant to Seychelles. Within the West Indies, this species is most common in the Lesser Antilles, Virgin Islands and small islands east of Puerto Rico. Breeding in the Western Palearctic occurs on the Cape Verde Islands and the Îles des Madeleines off Senegal

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: |last2=Young |title=The Red-billed Tropicbird Phaethon rubricauda in Hawaii, with notes on interspecific behavior of tropicbirds |journal=Marine Ornithology |volume=35 |year=2007 |pages=81–84 |url=https://www.researchgate.net/publication/237377707 |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180217202830/https://www.researchgate.net/profile/Lindsay_Young/publication/237377707_The_Red-billed_Tropicbird_Phaethon_rubricauda_in_Hawaii_with_notes_on_interspecific_behavior_of_tropicbirds/links/00b7d52c271783ce39000000.pdf |archive-date=2018-02-17 }} It sometimes wanders further, including five records from Great Britain, and two from Australia: October–December 2010 on Lord Howe Island and September 2014 on Ashmore Reef. In July 2005, one was found in eastern New Brunswick, Canada, likely the same bird that later visited Seal Island and Matinicus Rock, Maine every summer from 2005 to 2021.{{cite web |title=Why does this red-billed tropicbird keep returning to Maine?

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: These predators have the potential to drive populations of the red-billed tropicbird into serious decline. It is estimated that this bird experienced a population bottleneck about 450 to 750 years ago, likely due to exploitation by humans
